summary: Large visual-effects decomp pass focused on matching and naming: renamed the efSpecial unit to efAlt, moved shared ef static/small data into new efdata.c/h, renamed many efLib/efAsync/efSync APIs and structs, matched efAlt_Spawn plus several ef data sections, got efAsync_Spawn and efsync/efasync .sdata to 100%, and modestly improved efSync_Spawn and efAsync_Dispatch. The PR also updated callers across fighter/item/game-mode/VI/lbdvd/grlib/particle code and added typed math constants for matching.
what_changed: Renamed the effect unit from efSpecial to efAlt: configure.py and config/GALE01/splits.txt now build melee/ef/efalt.c instead of melee/ef/efspecial.c, efSpecial_SpawnSpecial became efAlt_Spawn, and efspecial.c/h were deleted while efalt.c/h were added.

Added efdata.c/h as the shared ef data owner. efLib_AllocData moved out of eflib_alloc.c, and efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, and efLib_AnimCount were defined in efdata.c/.sbss. eflib_alloc.c/h were removed and users now include ef/efdata.h.

Reworked efasync.c with descriptive names and stronger types: efAsync_80063930 became efAsync_Dispatch, load helpers became efAsync_LoadAsync/efAsync_OnLoad/efAsync_LoadSync, queue helpers became efAsync_QueueProcessDeferred/QueueFlush/QueueClear/QueueInit, and ef_Symbols became EF_DAT_Entry with efAsync_DatEntries.

Updated efAsync_Spawn around EF_QueuedEffect and EF_SpawnKind. Numeric spawn kinds were replaced with enum names such as EF_SPAWN_ATTACH, EF_SPAWN_POS_OFFSET_PARAM2, and EF_SPAWN_CAMERA_SHAKE, documenting how queued effects convert JObj positions and forward variadic parameters to efSync_Spawn.

Reworked efsync.c naming and flow. efSync_Spawn now documents gfx_id routing ranges, dispatches 0x0250-0x0477 through efAsync_Dispatch, dispatches 0x0479-0x04BA through efAlt_Spawn, and uses efLib_LoadKind/efLib_AnimCount instead of address-named sbss globals.

Renamed many efLib functions and data structures from address names to behavior names, including efLib_Init, efLib_SetFlags, efLib_Destroy, efLib_Create, efLib_Create_Attach, efLib_Create_AttachChild_Scale, efLib_CreateGenerator_AppSRT_SetFacingDir, efLib_Cb_* callbacks, efLib_AnimQueue, and efLib_ParamTable.

Renamed Effect and related fields in src/melee/ef/types.h to EF_Effect, EF_EffectDesc, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_DAT_Entry, EF_ParamEntry, with named fields such as next, parent_gobj, attach_jobj, update, user_data, params, lifetime, expire_flags, scale_flags, state_flags, and is_async.

Updated broad call sites to the new API names: fighters/items now use efAsync_QueueFlush and efAsync_QueueClear, fighter data loading uses efAsync_LoadAsync/LoadSync, lbdvd uses efAsync_OnLoad, and game/VI entry paths call efLib_Init plus efAsync_LoadSync.

Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt for descriptive symbols and data labels, including efAlt_Spawn, efAsync_Dispatch, efAsync_DatEntries, efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, efLib_AnimCount, efLib_AnimQueue, efLib_ParamTable, and efAlt_* small-data labels.

Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt section ownership: efdata.c owns efLib_AllocData .bss and ef .sbss, eflib no longer owns that .sbss range, efalt replaces efspecial, and efsync/efasync .sdata section ends were tightened.
Added M_PI_F, M_PI_2_F, M_PI_3_F, M_PI_L, M_TAU_L, M_PI_2_L, and M_PI_3_L to src/MSL/math.h.
Renamed particle/grlib argument names for clarity: hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC now expose linkNo, bank, and gfx_id parameters, and grLib_801C99C0 mirrors bank/gfx_id/jobj/flag naming.

decomp.dev reported matched code 59.22% (+656 bytes), 9 new matches, and 2 improved unmatched items. New matches included efAlt_Spawn, efalt .data/.sdata/.sdata2, efdata .bss/.sbss, efsync .sdata, efasync .sdata, and efAsync_Spawn. It also reported apparent broken matches for efspecial and eflib_alloc due to the rename/rehome.
smart_moves: Centralized cross-file ef allocation and small-data state in efdata.c/h instead of leaving globals scattered between eflib_alloc and eflib. This made section ownership explicit and allowed config splits to match the intended .bss/.sbss layout.

Recorded the MWCC small-data ordering hazard directly in efdata.c: the comment notes that .sbss may be allocated in reverse definition order, explaining why efLib_AnimCount, efLib_EffectCount, and efLib_LoadKind are defined in that source order.

Converted anonymous numeric switch cases in async queue processing into EF_SpawnKind enum values with comments describing argument flow. This makes variadic effect-spawn call sites much safer to reason about.
Used behavior-rich efLib names that encode effect creation variants, such as Attach, AttachChild, Scale, FacingDir, Pos, and AppSRT, instead of opaque address suffixes.
Documented efSync_Spawn routing by gfx_id range in the source, making it clear which IDs use generator creation, async dispatch, efAlt, or local sync switch cases.

Left TODOs around efSync_Spawn and efAsync_Dispatch jump-table state instead of pretending the remaining mismatch was solved. The author and source comments both identify jump tables as the likely remaining blocker.
Updated symbols, splits, configure.py, headers, and call sites together, avoiding a half-renamed ef API where old address symbols and new typed code would coexist.

Preserved matching pragmatism: eflib.c contains a TODO saying the original source may have only had eflib.c and efasync.c, but the current split was kept for matching-file percentage until better evidence exists.
decomp_lessons: When moving matched data between objects, update splits.txt and symbols.txt in the same change. Otherwise a true rehome can appear as broken matches in the decomp.dev report.
For MWCC .sbss work, verify definition order empirically. This PR explicitly used reverse-order placement to land efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, and efLib_AnimCount at the expected addresses.
Variadic dispatchers should be typed incrementally and documented. efAlt_Spawn and efSync_Spawn still pass va_list through, but named parameters and route comments make future matching safer.

Jump-table data regressions can be tied to the parent switch function. The author stated the displayed issue was jumptable-related and expected it to be inherently fixed once the associated function matches.

Descriptive renames should include struct fields, flags, data labels, and call sites, not just function symbols. EF_Effect field names and EF_STATE/EF_SCALE/EF_LOADKIND constants made the efLib rewrites much clearer.
Keep array sizes and static asserts when renaming data-table types. EF_DAT_Entry preserved the 0xC size and 51-entry effect DAT table while replacing unk8 with data.
If a previous unit name was wrong or stale, a rename can show as both new matches and broken matches. Here efSpecial became efAlt, so efalt reached 100% while efspecial disappeared.
Adding correctly suffixed math constants can reduce accidental type promotion or small-data constant mismatches in effect math-heavy code.
naming_conventions: Module prefix retained: efLib_, efAsync_, efSync_, efAlt_.
Descriptive public efLib verbs use Pascal-style suffixes after the prefix: efLib_Init, efLib_SetFlags, efLib_Destroy, efLib_Create, efLib_CreateGenerator.
Creation variants are encoded with underscore-separated qualifiers: efLib_Create_Attach, efLib_Create_AttachChild, efLib_Create_Attach_Scale_FacingDir, efLib_CreateGenerator_AppSRT_SetFacingDirScale.
Callback symbols use Cb_ after the module prefix: efLib_Cb_SPtcl, efLib_Cb_DPtcl, efLib_Cb_SetScale_FromParamX, efLib_Cb_ftMr_SpecialLw.
Lowercase internal/proc-style names remain for some non-public helpers: efLib_remove_user_data, efLib_render_callback, efLib_particles_proc_main, efLib_particles_proc_aux.
Struct/type names use EF_ prefix and descriptive nouns: EF_Effect, EF_EffectDesc, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_DAT_Entry, EF_ParamEntry, EF_SpawnKind.
Enum and flag constants are uppercase and grouped by purpose: EF_SPAWN_*, EF_LOADKIND_*, EF_SCALE_*, EF_DOES_*, EF_STATE_*.
Loaded DAT table fields were named for content: ef_DAT_file, effDataTable_name, data.
Particle API argument names were clarified as linkNo, bank, and gfx_id.
The author explicitly said the new descriptive ef names were tentative and could be changed, so future work should treat them as useful but not final if stronger evidence appears.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: New efdata.c owns efLib_AllocData in .bss and efLib_LoadKind/EffectCount/AnimCount in .sbss; splits.txt assigns .sbss start 0x804D64E8 end 0x804D64F4 to efdata.c.
efdata.c comments that MWCC may allocate .sbss in reverse definition order; definitions were ordered to produce addresses 0x804D64F0, 0x804D64EC, and 0x804D64E8.
efAlt_Spawn uses EFALT_VA_ARG(t), based on __va_arg and _var_arg_typeof(t), to consume the incoming va_list consistently while matching the switch-heavy spawner.
efSync_Spawn and efAsync_Dispatch retained TODO comments with current approximate match percentages and jump-table percentages, making remaining mismatch scope explicit.
PAD_STACK adjustments and more exact local variable typing were used throughout efasync.c and efsync.c while preserving variadic call behavior.
eflib.c forward-declared HSD_MtxGetScale and grLib_801C99C0 to avoid sdata2 pollution, according to the source comment.
M_PI_F and M_PI_L/M_TAU_L constants were added so source can choose float or long-double literal forms instead of relying only on M_PI/M_TAU.
decomp.dev showed efAsync_Spawn moving to 100% and efSync_Spawn improving from 95.90% to 98.28%, while efAsync_Dispatch improved from 98.46% to 98.60%.
decomp.dev also reported efasync .data regression, and the author attributed a visible issue to a jumptable that should be fixed when its associated function matches.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported 9 new matches, 6 broken matches, 2 improvements in unmatched items, and one efasync .data regression.
The broken matches were mostly expected fallout from renaming/reowning units: efspecial became efalt and eflib_alloc data moved to efdata.
Author comment on an attached image: the issue was jumptable-related and should be inherently fixed once the associated function matches.
Author stated the descriptive ef-related names were not final: 'Feel free to change any of these, I am not attached to them.'
follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining efSync_Spawn jump table mismatch and whether matching the switch fixes jumptable_803BF870.
Investigate efAsync_Dispatch .data/jumptable regression and the reported jump table around jumptable_803BFB28.
Search for stale efSpecial or efLib_8005*/efAsync_80067* references after this rename pass.
Verify whether original source evidence supports consolidating efalt/efsync into eflib.c or keeping the split for match tracking.
Audit EF_Effect flag semantics: expire_flags, scale_flags, state_flags, is_async, and lifetime.
Check all game-mode and VI entry paths that load common effects to ensure efLib_Init precedes efAsync_LoadSync(0).
Reuse efdata.c .sbss ordering tactic for other small-data matching problems, but verify order per unit.
Review hsd_8039EFAC/hsd_8039F6CC argument naming against particle system evidence.
